errors.fellOffEdge: Oh no - you tried to fall off the edge of the maze!
errors.hitWall: Ouch - you walked into a wall!

checks.reachedEnd: You didn't reach the end of the maze.
checks.extraTurn: You seem to have done an extra unnecessary turn at the end.

straightPath.name: A straight path
straightPath.description: Move forward to the end of the maze.

turnLeft.name: Turn left if you can
turnLeft.description: If there's a path to the left, take it!

turnRight.name: Turn right if you can't move straight or left
turnRight.description: If there's not a path to the left or straight ahead, take the path to the right.

turnAround.name: Turn around if needed
turnAround.description: Handle dead ends by turning around. If you can't go left, straight, or right, turn around and move forward.

maze1.name: Guide person to the end of the maze

forks.name: Choose left if you can, otherwise choose right
forks.description: A maze with forks that tests left-priority

turnAround.name: Turn around at a dead end
turnAround.description: A maze that requires turning around

forks2.name: Complex maze with forks and dead ends
forks2.description: A complex maze testing the full algorithm

solveEachMaze.question: Do I have to solve each maze separately?
solveEachMaze.answer: No. You write one algorithm that works for any maze, and the same code runs on every scenario.

runEveryStep.question: How do I get my code to run for every step of the maze?
runEveryStep.answer: Wrap your logic in the repeat loop. It runs your code over and over until you reach the end, so you only need to describe what to do for a single step.

oneCheckPerStep.question: How do I make sure only one of my checks happens each step?
oneCheckPerStep.answer: Use a single `if` / `else if` / `else` chain rather than several separate `if`s. That way exactly one branch runs each time through the loop.

stuckAfterTurn.question: I turn, but the character gets stuck in the same spot.
stuckAfterTurn.answer: Turning only changes the direction the character faces, it doesn't take a step. After turning, you still need to `move()`.

turnAroundDeadEnd.question: How do I turn around at a dead end?
turnAroundDeadEnd.answer: There's no turn-around function. Turning the same way twice (two left turns, or two right turns) leaves the character facing back the way it came.

move.description: Moves the character **one step forward** in the current direction. The character will only move if the target position is not blocked by a wall.

turnLeft.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the left** (counterclockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

turnRight.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the right** (clockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

canMove.description: Returns **true** if the space ahead of the character is not a wall, **false** otherwise.

canTurnLeft.description: Returns **true** if the space to the character's left is not a wall, **false** otherwise.

canTurnRight.description: Returns **true** if the space to the character's right is not a wall, **false** otherwise.
